proof_of_work.payload_mode_edge_case:
Simulated consumer repo with repo-root tools/README.md at version 9.0.0 and a .nizam/ payload whose tools/README.md is version 1.0.0. Ran bash ../tools/validate.sh --payload from inside .nizam/. C8 correctly skipped .nizam/tools/README.md because git show HEAD:.nizam/tools/README.md found no tracked file, avoiding the false downgrade failure against the repo-root tools/README.md. Output:
MODE: payload (validating consumer-injected subset; framework-envelope checks skipped)
[C1] PASS frontmatter-schema
[C2] PASS format
[C3] PASS untagged-fence-sweep
[C4] PASS index-integrity
[C5] PASS branding-leakage
[C6] SKIP bootstrap-sanity (payload mode)
[C7] PASS module-readme-presence
[C8] PASS version-bump-vs-changelog
SUMMARY (payload mode): 7 passed, 0 failed
proof_of_work.target_mode:
bash tools/validate.sh --target standard/NDS.md
[C1] PASS frontmatter-schema
[C2] PASS format
[C3] PASS untagged-fence-sweep
proof_of_work.code_change: tools/validate.sh: check_c8_version_changelog() now computes git_prefix=$(git rev-parse --show-prefix 2>/dev/null || echo "") and calls git show "HEAD:${git_prefix}${f}" so payload-mode paths resolve relative to the repo root.
